Spring Data for Apache Cassandra Core

    1. insert(Class)

Interface ReactiveInsertOperation

All Known Subinterfaces:
ReactiveCassandraOperations, ReactiveFluentCassandraOperations
All Known Implementing Classes:
ReactiveCassandraTemplate
public interface ReactiveInsertOperation
The ReactiveInsertOperation interface allows creation and execution of Cassandra INSERT operations in a fluent API style.

By default,the table to operate on is derived from the initial domainType and can be defined there via Table annotation. Using inTable allows a developer to override the table name for the execution. 

    
        insert(Jedi.class)
            .inTable("star_wars")
            .one(luke);
Since:
2.1
Author:
Mark Paluch, John Blum

    Description
    static interface 
    ReactiveInsertOperation.InsertWithOptions<T>
    Apply InsertOptions (optional).
    static interface 
    ReactiveInsertOperation.InsertWithTable<T>
    Table override (optional).
    static interface 
    ReactiveInsertOperation.ReactiveInsert<T>
    The ReactiveInsertOperation.ReactiveInsert interface provides methods for constructing INSERT operations in a fluent way.
    static interface 
    ReactiveInsertOperation.TerminatingInsert<T>
    Trigger INSERT execution by calling one of the terminating methods.

    <T> ReactiveInsertOperation.ReactiveInsert<T>
    insert(Class<T> domainType)
    Begin creating an INSERT operation for given domainType.

  • Method Details

    • insert

      <T> ReactiveInsertOperation.ReactiveInsert<T> insert(Class<T> domainType)
      Begin creating an INSERT operation for given domainType.
      Type Parameters:
      T - type of the application domain object.
      Parameters:
      domainType - type of the domain object to insert; must not be null.
      Returns:
      new instance of ReactiveInsertOperation.ReactiveInsert.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if domainType is null.
      See Also:
      • ReactiveInsertOperation.ReactiveInsert
